scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed handoff summary with explicit owner chain, readiness state, and required artifacts.
The specialist blocked booking until permit, site, access, and utility prerequisites were explicit.
Overlapping owner claims were surfaced as a routing conflict instead of being silently absorbed.
Closure was withheld until inspection outcomes and closeout deliverables were linked to the record.
Licensed trade and code-interpretation authority were refused and routed outward.
The lane preserved coordination-only posture and returned execution work to the operational owner.
Low-confidence escalation named missing tenant systems, owner maps, and jurisdictional context before any routing commitment.
Conflicting public and tenant rules on sign-off timing were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported authority.
Construction-specific terminology was normalized into the correct artifacts and routing posture.
Safety and inspection changes correctly blocked the schedule and produced an escalation path.
Closure confirmation required destination-owner proof and complete permit or inspection context.
Tenant-specific role names and queue terms were adapted without losing baseline permit, safety, inspection, or closeout controls.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and both construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
